prompt: Please do the task stated in `/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2_generation_prompts.md`, where the TASK_ID is `da6e95e5`.
codex_response:
Implemented the `da6e95e5` ARC2 package. The rule is: find the local TL/TR/BL/BR corner-marker quartet whose enclosed payload contains the same color as the markers, then extract that payload’s bounding box while preserving background cells inside it. The verifier passed all 5 official examples, the builder produced 1000 generated examples, `./arc2_verify_generated_all.sh --task da6e95e5` passed `1000/1000`, and I visually sanity-checked [preview_01.png](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/da6e95e5/previews/preview_01.png).

I used the Opus hint as a starting point and discarded the Sonnet hint; the mismatch is documented in [notes.md](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_da6e95e5/notes.md). No new primitive was needed in `arc2/dsl_ext.py`. The generator matches the family by building 30×30 uniform-background scenes with multiple size-2 or size-3 corner-marker frames and exactly one same-color target payload.
